name: MavSocial
description:
What MavSocial Does

MavSocial is a social media and reputation management platform that supports LinkedIn and most other social platforms. MavSocial excels in publishing visual content.

With MavSocial, you can create ad campaigns on LinkedIn and other social platforms.

Source: MavSocial

Key Features

The key features of MavSocial include: 

  • The ability to create ad campaigns on LinkedIn and other social platforms. 
  • Functionalities for controlling ad expenditure, campaign timeframes, and audience.
  • MavSocial excels in visual content management. 
  • Other features include: some text
    • A bulk post-uploader 
    • Social content calendar, review management, and social listening tools.
    • Advertisement comment monitoring
    • Competitor analysis.

Benefits of MavSocial

  • Create ad campaigns on LinkedIn and other social platforms. 
  • Control and edit: some text
    •  your ad spend and budget limits, 
    • campaign timeframes, and 
    • audience
  • Manage visual content with Mavsocial: host, manage, access, edit, publish, and analyze. 
  • Bulk scheduling and uploading posts on Twitter and other social platforms.  
  • Track and manage reviews with the review management and social listening features.

Pricing

Four plans are available: 

  • Advance: USD 29 / month some text
    • One user
    • Ten profiles  
  • Pro: USD 78 /month  some text
    • Three users 
    • 30 profiles   
  • Business:  USD 249 /month  some text
    • Five users 
    • 40 profiles 
  • Enterprise: USD 399 /monthsome text
    • Customized features 

Pros

✅ Create ad campaigns on LinkedIn and other social platforms

✅ Manage visual content 

✅ White labeling

✅ 24/7 access to the platform via the mobile app.

Cons

🚫Manual interference is required for some activities 

🚫Not user-friendly (especially for new users)
what_people_say:
Read, Choose, and Get Started. 

Did any of these tools sound like a good fit?  

Selecting the right tool for your business is not an overnight decision. Take your time to think through things. Bookmark this post so you can come back whenever you need to clarify any issue. 

Once you’ve chosen and set up your new tool, you will find many ways to improved LinkedIn engagement. Most importantly, you will save time in managing your LinkedIn activities and focus more on other  critical aspects of your business.   

So, go ahead and pick a tool with the right set of features for you and supercharge your LinkedIn campaign today with well-timed and highly engaging posts.
summing_up_description:
Get started with Synup Social.

At Synup, we have the know-how, software solutions, and passion to help businesses enhance their brand and elevate their turnover. 

Why Synup? 

  1. Proven results—We take pride in our customers' success; our proven results and case studies testify to this.
  2. We form partnerships where our customers share their pain points, and our team helps them find permanent solutions with expert support.   
  3. Our managed services put you ahead of the game. Tap into years of experience and use the resources that help us drive results.
  4. Synup offers industry-leading, innovative solutions to save time and improve business turnover. Our automated tools will do the tedious work while you focus on managing your business. 
  5. White-label solutions allow agencies to own their marketing campaigns with all-around branding.

Best LinkedIn Scheduler: FAQs

  1. What are the best times to post on LinkedIn?

The best times to post on LinkedIn are: 

  • Monday: 11 AM.
  • Tuesday: 6 AM to 8 AM.
  • Wednesday: 12 PM.
  • Thursday: 2 PM.
  • Friday: 8 AM.
  • Saturday: 4 AM to 5 AM.
  • Sunday: 6 PM.
  1. How long should a LinkedIn Post be?

The recommended length for a LinkedIn post is 1300 to 2000 words. Additionally, short posts of 15 - 300 words perform well too. 

  1. How many posts can you schedule on LinkedIn per day?

You should post one post per day on LinkedIn. If you want to post two posts daily, ensure at least seven hours between the two posts. If you post your second post too soon, it may affect the previous posts' reach. 

  1. Which posts are most successful on LinkedIn? 

Although there is no solid data on the topic, the following categories of posts are most repeated on LinkedIn:

  • Career milestone: Posts where you celebrate a work promotion. an anniversary, or a career change.
  • The humble brag: Posts where you highlight a recent achievement of yourself, a colleague, or an organization.
  • Leadership story: Posts about sharing workplace insights where you add a personal experience.
  • Upcoming events: Posts where you promote a happening, such as a product launch or webinar.
  • Expert opinion: Posts that comment on a breaking news story.
